violations.text: Per interview of staff, slicers are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer and ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of. Ice machine and slicer were cleaned during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

Multiple food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023
Onions were observed stored on a dirty shelf. Contamination of food must be prevented during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023
